Run M1 walk-forward predictions on training seasons

Source: R/m2_loso_fold.R
nested_loso_m1_train.Rd

Generates M1 stacking features for all training seasons in a fold. Calls m1_walkforward_multi() using the fold's reference curve and hyperparams.

Usage

nested_loso_m1_train(
  allD,
  fold,
  params,
  horizons = c(1L, 2L),
  allow_scale = NULL,
  use_ci = TRUE,
  buffer_weeks = 0L,
  min_obs = 4L,
  curvature_ratio = 1,
  temperature = 0.25,
  rise_weight = 1,
  trough_weight = 0.1,
  peak_decay = 0.3,
  slope_weight = 8,
  slope_window = 6L,
  dynamic_temp = FALSE,
  dynamic_temp_pivot = 10L,
  top_k = NULL,
  blend_alpha = 1,
  parallel = TRUE,
  verbose = TRUE
)

Arguments

allD

Full multi-season data frame.

fold

Output of nested_loso_build_fold().

params

M0 detection parameters.

horizons

Integer vector of forecast horizons (default c(1L, 2L)).

allow_scale

Passed to m1_walkforward_multi().

use_ci

Logical; peak CI control (default TRUE).

buffer_weeks

Integer; peak buffer (default 0L).

min_obs

Integer; minimum rows for alignment (default 4L).

curvature_ratio

Numeric; delta curvature gate (default 1.0).

temperature, rise_weight, trough_weight, peak_decay

Ensemble and alignment-loss controls.

slope_weight, slope_window

Growth-rate similarity controls.

dynamic_temp, dynamic_temp_pivot

Early-season temperature controls.

top_k, blend_alpha

Template filtering and blending controls.

parallel

Logical; run M1 seasons in parallel (default TRUE).

verbose

Logical; print progress.

Value

Tibble of M1 walk-forward predictions for training seasons (columns: season, eval_weekF, target_weekF, h, m1_p_hat, ...). Can be empty if no ignition detected.
